meta.note: Results are a combination of dynamic test execution (backend: node --test, frontend: vitest run) and static analysis of component and store code. Performance results are derived from static latency spec review and spec-declared thresholds — no live k6 run was executed. Security results are from static code review against OWASP Top 10.

summary.coverage_notes: Backend: 31/32 integration tests pass (1 fails due to SETTINGS_FILE isolation bug). Frontend: 354/354 unit/component tests pass, but AgentRunIndicator, AgentSettingsPanel, PipelineProgressBar, useAgentCompletion, and the launcher store slice have zero test coverage — estimated frontend coverage for new launcher code is ~71%.

failed_tests.name: returns 200 with default settings when no settings file exists
failed_tests.error: Assertion failed: default fileInputMethod should be cat-subshell
failed_tests.root_cause: SETTINGS_FILE is hardcoded to path.join(DEFAULT_DATA_DIR, 'settings.json') at module scope. The test server uses an isolated tmpDir via startTestServer(), but SETTINGS_FILE still points to the real data/settings.json, which was left with fileInputMethod='stdin-redirect' from a prior test run.

warnings: act() warnings in DeleteSpaceDialog.test.tsx (4 tests) — pre-existing, not related to agent-launcher
act() warnings in AttachmentModal.test.tsx (1 test) — pre-existing
act() warnings in ConfigPanel.test.tsx (multiple tests) — pre-existing
console.log in executeAgentRun() outputs JSON to console in test runs — Low quality issue
coverage_gaps: useAppStore: launcher slice actions (prepareAgentRun, executeAgentRun, cancelAgentRun, startPipeline, advancePipeline, abortPipeline, loadSettings, saveSettings) — not tested

performance.static_assessment: All endpoints use synchronous fs I/O (readdirSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync). For the typical local use case (< 20 agent files, < 10KB prompt text), latency thresholds should be met. Under high concurrency or large agent directories (100+ files), synchronous I/O on the main thread may cause queuing. No live k6 run was executed — performance tests marked as skipped.
performance.cleanup_assessment: cleanupOldPromptFiles() removes files older than 24h. Called on startup. Periodic cleanup interval not observed in server code — files accumulate between restarts if the server runs continuously for > 24h without restart.

owasp_checks.notes: AGENT_ID_RE = /^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$/ blocks all non-kebab chars including dots and slashes before file access. Test TC-031 confirms 400/404 response, never 200.

owasp_checks.check: Path traversal — resolved path must be inside AGENTS_DIR
owasp_checks.notes: handleGetAgent uses path.resolve() + startsWith(AGENTS_DIR + path.sep) guard. This is sound — path.normalize anti-pattern is NOT present here; the guard uses the fully resolved path against a known safe prefix.

owasp_checks.check: workingDirectory in POST /api/v1/agent/prompt can reference arbitrary filesystem paths
owasp_checks.notes: workingDirectory is written into the prompt text file as a label string (not executed). No file access is performed on it. Low risk — informational only, included in prompt for the agent to read.

owasp_checks.check: Shell metacharacters in task title/description injected into prompt file
owasp_checks.notes: Prompt content is written to a temp file, not to a shell command string. The cliCommand references the file via $(cat /path) — shell metacharacters in task data remain inside the file and are not interpolated into the command string itself.

owasp_checks.check: cli.binary in settings stored and used in CLI command
owasp_checks.notes: cli.binary is used as the command prefix in buildCliCommand (e.g. 'mycli -p ...'). The server never shell-executes this string — it is returned to the client for terminal injection. The user controls the terminal, so this is not a server-side injection vector. However, a malicious value like 'rm -rf / ; claude' injected via PUT /api/v1/settings would be stored and returned in subsequent prompt responses. The endpoint has no auth (by design — local only). Documented as advisory for awareness.

The api-spec.json specifies maxLength:2000 for customInstructions. The server handler (handleGeneratePrompt) does not validate or truncate customInstructions length. An arbitrarily large payload causes the prompt file to grow proportionally. Filed as BUG-004.
AgentPromptPreview uses React's {preparedRun.cliCommand} in a <pre> element. React auto-escapes all text content. No innerHTML usage detected for cliCommand or promptPreview display.

New endpoints /api/v1/agents, /api/v1/agent/prompt, /api/v1/settings return JSON responses without security headers. This is a pre-existing pattern across the entire server. Filed as BUG-005 (pre-existing).

owasp_checks.check: Error responses never expose stack traces or internal paths
owasp_checks.notes: sendError() constructs responses from explicit code/message/suggestion strings. Stack traces are logged to console but not included in API responses.
Static file serving is limited to dist/ (the built frontend). data/.prompts/ is not in the static path. Direct HTTP access to prompt files is not possible.

owasp_checks.check: No authentication required (by design — local only)
owasp_checks.notes: API spec explicitly states local-only, no authentication required. All endpoints bind to localhost:3000. Acceptable for a local developer tool.

owasp_checks.category: A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures
owasp_checks.check: Atomic write pattern for settings.json and prompt files
owasp_checks.notes: Both writeSettings() and handleGeneratePrompt use .tmp + rename atomic pattern. Race condition risk on prompt write is minimal (unique timestamp+taskId filenames).

owasp_checks.category: A09:2021 Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
owasp_checks.check: Structured JSON logging for agent_prompt_generated events
owasp_checks.notes: Structured log emitted on each prompt generation with agentId, taskId, spaceId, promptPath, estimatedTokens. Adequate for local audit trail.
Feature implemented using only existing dependencies (Node.js built-ins, existing React/Zustand stack). ADR confirms zero new dependencies.
